skill.name: scikit-learn
skill.description: Guidance for building classical machine learning workflows with scikit-learn, including preprocessing, supervised learning, clustering, model evaluation, tuning, and reusable pipelines.
skill.summary: Build and evaluate scikit-learn machine learning workflows in Python.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: AI review dismisses the critical heuristic and most static findings as false positives caused by markdown code fences, normal Python imports, ML terminology, and official documentation links. No prompt injection evidence was found in targeted review. The remaining real concerns are low-risk local artifact writes and serialized model loading examples that require trusted inputs.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Import Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The large external command and dynamic import result set is a false positive. The cited lines are markdown code fences, ordinary Python import statements, or scikit-learn API names, not hidden shell execution or dynamic code loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled locations show fenced documentation examples and normal import statements. No subprocess, eval, exec, or user-controlled dynamic import was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: High-Severity Keyword Matches Are ML Terminology
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and C2 keyword alerts are false positives from machine learning terms such as scaling, encoding, SGD, and PCA axis labels. The reviewed lines do not contain cryptographic logic, command-and-control behavior, or credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited high-severity examples are benign scikit-learn concepts or plotting labels. They do not show malicious networking, cryptography misuse, or remote control semantics.

low_findings.title: Network Indicators Are Official Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are resource links to official scikit-learn documentation. No reviewed code sends data to external services or performs runtime network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are static documentation links. I did not find evidence of credential exfiltration, telemetry, or programmatic network calls.

low_findings.title: Examples Write Local Artifacts
low_findings.description: Some examples write local files, including clustering plot images, cached pipeline directories, and model persistence files. This is expected for ML workflows, but users should run examples in a working directory where these artifacts are acceptable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence shows local file writes and temporary directory creation. The filenames are fixed examples, with no evidence of destructive writes or sensitive path targeting.

low_findings.title: Model Deserialization Examples Need Trusted Inputs
low_findings.description: The reference material includes pickle and joblib model loading examples. This is common in scikit-learn, but loading untrusted serialized model files can execute unsafe code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly show pickle.load and joblib.load model persistence examples. Risk depends on whether users load untrusted files, so this is a documentation warning rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Examples Write Local Artifacts
dangerous_patterns.description: Some examples write local files, including clustering plot images, cached pipeline directories, and model persistence files. This is expected for ML workflows, but users should run examples in a working directory where these artifacts are acceptable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence shows local file writes and temporary directory creation. The filenames are fixed examples, with no evidence of destructive writes or sensitive path targeting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Model Deserialization Examples Need Trusted Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference material includes pickle and joblib model loading examples. This is common in scikit-learn, but loading untrusted serialized model files can execute unsafe code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly show pickle.load and joblib.load model persistence examples. Risk depends on whether users load untrusted files, so this is a documentation warning rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Classical machine learning projects need clear choices for preprocessing, modeling, and evaluation. This skill provides practical scikit-learn patterns for reliable Python ML workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains supervised learning choices for classification and regression tasks.
Shows preprocessing patterns for numeric, categorical, missing, and scaled data.
Provides pipeline and ColumnTransformer examples for repeatable workflows.
Covers cross-validation, metrics, and hyperparameter tuning with scikit-learn.
Includes clustering, PCA, and model comparison examples.
Provides reusable Python scripts for classification and clustering analysis.
limitations: Does not train models without user-provided data and execution context.
Focuses on classical scikit-learn workflows, not deep learning frameworks.
Example scripts may write local plot files when run.
Model persistence examples require trusted model files and careful environment control.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Classification Model
use_cases.description: Select preprocessing, compare baseline classifiers, and evaluate results with appropriate metrics.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reusable ML Pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine imputers, encoders, scalers, and estimators into maintainable scikit-learn pipelines.

use_cases.title: Teach Classical ML Concepts
use_cases.description: Use concise references for algorithms, clustering, dimensionality reduction, and validation concepts.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a First Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a scikit-learn baseline model for this dataset and target. Explain the preprocessing steps I need.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Preprocessing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scikit-learn preprocessing plan for numeric, categorical, and missing values in my dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Model Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my machine learning task and recommend cross-validation, scoring metrics, and error analysis steps.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Production Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete scikit-learn pipeline with hyperparameter search, leakage prevention, and model comparison.

output_examples.input: I have customer churn data with numeric usage columns and categorical plan columns.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a ColumnTransformer, imputation, one-hot encoding, scaling where useful, and a baseline classifier comparison.

output_examples.input: I need to evaluate an imbalanced binary classifier.
output_examples.output: The response suggests stratified validation, precision, recall, F1, ROC AUC, PR AUC, and threshold analysis.

output_examples.input: I want to cluster users and explain the segments.
output_examples.output: The response outlines scaling, PCA review, K selection, silhouette analysis, clustering comparison, and segment interpretation.

best_practices: Fit preprocessing only on training data to avoid leakage.
Use pipelines when cross-validating preprocessing and model steps together.
Choose metrics that match the business cost of false positives and false negatives.
anti_patterns: Do not scale or encode the full dataset before splitting train and test data.
Do not rely on accuracy alone for imbalanced classification problems.
Do not load pickled or joblib model files from untrusted sources.

faq.question: Does this skill run scikit-learn automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and example scripts that users can choose to run in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with both classification and regression?
faq.answer: Yes. The references cover common supervised learning models, metrics, and tuning patterns.

faq.question: Does it support clustering workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes clustering, PCA, algorithm comparison, and cluster visualization guidance.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include production deployment guidance?
faq.answer: It focuses on pipelines, evaluation, and persistence basics, not full deployment infrastructure.

faq.question: Are saved model files always safe to load?
faq.answer: No. Only load pickle or joblib model files from trusted sources and controlled environments.
